boogie/stores/kvstore

        pairsUnordered
      • pairsUnordered(s: KvStore): (string, string)

This module implements a simple key-value store with optional write-ahead logging (WAL) and disk persistence. It uses an in-memory hash map for fast lookups and a sorted index for ordered iteration. The WAL allows for durability and crash recovery, while periodic checkpoints can be taken to speed up recovery time.

Imports

../wal

Types

KvStorageMode = enum
  ksmInMemory, ksmDisk
Defines the storage mode for the key-value store. ksmInMemory creates a purely in-memory store with no persistence, while ksmDisk creates a file-based store that can persist data across restarts 
KvStore {.acyclic.} = ref object
The main data structure for the key-value store 
KvStoreError = object of CatchableError
A catchable exception type for errors related to the key-value store operations.

Procs

proc checkpoint(s: KvStore) {....raises: [IOError, WalError, OSError, Exception], tags: [
    WriteIOEffect, ReadDirEffect, WriteDirEffect, ReadIOEffect], forbids: [].}
Forces a checkpoint (snapshot) to be taken immediately. This can be used to ensure that all operations up to the current LSN are persisted to disk, which can speed up recovery time in case of a crash. If WAL is enabled, the WAL is flushed before taking the snapshot to ensure durability. 
proc delete(s: KvStore; key: string): bool {.discardable,
    ...raises: [IOError, WalError, OSError, Exception], tags: [TimeEffect,
    WriteIOEffect, ReadDirEffect, WriteDirEffect, ReadIOEffect], forbids: [].}

Deletes the given key from the store. If WAL is enabled, the delete operation is first appended to the WAL before being applied to the in-memory store.

Returns true if the key was found and deleted, false if the key was not found.

proc get(s: KvStore; key: string): Option[string] {....raises: [KeyError],
    tags: [], forbids: [].}
Retrieves the value for the given key, if it exists. Returns none if the key is not found. 
proc hasKey(s: KvStore; key: string): bool {....raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
Checks if the given key exists in the store. 
proc isEmpty(s: KvStore): bool {....raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
Checks if the store is empty (contains no key-value pairs). 
proc len(s: KvStore): int {....raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
Returns the number of key-value pairs currently stored. 
proc newInMemoryKvStore(): KvStore {....raises: [KvStoreError, IOError, WalError,
    OSError, Exception], tags: [ReadDirEffect, WriteIOEffect, ReadIOEffect,
                                RootEffect], forbids: [].}
Creates a new in-memory key-value store with no persistence or WAL. This is useful for testing or scenarios where durability is not required. 
proc newKvStore(path: string; mode: KvStorageMode = ksmDisk;
                enableWal: bool = true; checkpointEveryOps: uint32 = 0'u32;
                walFlushEveryOps: uint32 = 1000'u32): KvStore {.
    ...raises: [KvStoreError, IOError, WalError, OSError, Exception],
    tags: [ReadDirEffect, WriteIOEffect, ReadIOEffect, RootEffect], forbids: [].}
Creates a new key-value store. If mode is ksmDisk, a file-based store is created at the given path. If enableWal is true, write-ahead logging is enabled for durability. The checkpointEveryOps parameter controls how often a checkpoint (snapshot) is taken after a certain number of operations, while walFlushEveryOps controls how often the WAL is flushed to disk. 
proc put(s: KvStore; key, value: string) {.
    ...raises: [IOError, WalError, KvStoreError, OSError, Exception], tags: [
    TimeEffect, WriteIOEffect, ReadDirEffect, WriteDirEffect, ReadIOEffect],
    forbids: [].}
Inserts or updates the value for the given key. If WAL is enabled, the operation is first appended to the WAL before being applied to the in-memory store. The checkpoint LSN is updated accordingly. 
proc recoverFromWal(s: KvStore) {....raises: [IOError, KvStoreError, WalError,
    OSError, Exception], tags: [ReadDirEffect, ReadIOEffect, WriteIOEffect,
                                WriteDirEffect], forbids: [].}

Iterators

iterator pairsUnordered(s: KvStore): (string, string) {....raises: [], tags: [],
    forbids: [].}
Iterates over all key-value pairs in the store in no particular order.
